items.title: FASTag Rules Explained: Can you use one FASTag for multiple vehicles? Check inside
items.summary: One FASTag can be used for one vehicle only because the VRN (Vehicle Registration Number is linked to one FASTag only. This FASTag is mapped to that vehicle in the National Electronic Toll Collection (NETC) system. Therefore, each vehicle must have its own separate FASTag. Even a family with different cars can't use the same FASTag for different vehicles.

The latest shareholding pattern also showed the promoter holding under Rakesh Jhunjhunwala and Associates fell to nil. Star Health is a Chennai-based health insurer.

items.title: RBI links broad-based edible oil price rise to biofuel use, cites Indonesia's B50 mandate
items.summary: RBI has linked broad-based rise in edible oil prices to growing biofuel demand, with Indonesia's higher palm oil blending mandate emerging as a key factor.

items.summary: Dalal Street saw continued declines, with major indices closing lower for the fourth session. Escalating Middle East tensions and rising crude oil prices contributed to fragile market sentiment, while private sector banks struggled and the Nifty 50 fell to 23,869.

items.title: Wipro buys TTK’s Good Home, Eva Brands for Rs 256 crore
items.summary: Wipro Consumer Care is acquiring TTK Healthcare's Good Home and Eva brands for Rs 256 crore. This strategic move expands Wipro's home and personal care product portfolios significantly. The acquired brands reported combined revenues of Rs 148 crore in the last fiscal year. Wipro plans to invest in distribution, innovation, and marketing for these established brands. This acquisition marks Wipro Consumer Care's seventeenth successful brand acquisition to date.

items.title: Food processing PLI attracts ₹9,207 crore investment, creates 3.35 lakh jobs
items.summary: The government said the scheme has boosted food processing capacity, product sales and exports while supporting MSMEs, innovation and value addition across the sector

items.title: D P Abhushan posts 77% rise in Q1 PAT to Rs 64 crore
items.summary: The company posted a PAT of Rs 36.42 crore in Q1 FY26, D P Abhushan said in a statement. Total income for the quarter rose 58% to Rs 854 crore from Rs 541 crore in the June quarter of the financial year ended March 31, 2026, the company said.

items.title: Bombay High Court grants interim relief to Sapat International in trademark case
items.summary: The Bombay High Court has granted interim relief to Sapat International in a trademark dispute over the century-old SAPAT brand, restraining another branch of the founding Joshi family from using the mark for tea products while selling goods under its IRAVI label.

items.title: Crypto deposits outpace withdrawals by up to 6x in H1; millennials dominate India's investor base: Report
items.summary: According to WazirX's H1 2026 India Crypto Trends Report, crypto deposits exceeded withdrawals by 2–6 times during the first half of 2026, while withdrawal requests fell nearly 55%, signalling stronger investor conviction and longer holding periods. The report also highlighted rising adoption of futures trading, strong participation from non-metro users and growing engagement across AI, DeFi and real-world asset tokens.

items.title: Adani's nuclear power ambition hinges on a green light
items.summary: Adani Power awaits government rules for private nuclear sector entry.  The company plans ten gigawatts of nuclear capacity by twenty thirty-five.  Investment decisions depend on regulatory clarity and cost-effectiveness for consumers.  Feasibility studies for potential sites in Madhya Pradesh are underway.  India aims to significantly expand nuclear power capacity by two thousand forty-seven.

items.title: Sensex, Nifty 50 fall for 4th consecutive session; investors lose  ₹3 lakh crore in a day; oil prices hit $99 per barrel
items.summary: The Sensex dropped 364 points, or 0.47%, to end at 76,391.39, while the Nifty 50 finished at 23,869.60, down 127 points, or 0.53%. In these four sessions, the Sensex has crashed 1,760 points, or 2.3%, while the NSE counterpart has shed 465 points, or almost 2%.

items.title: Oil prices near $100 a barrel: Analysts see risks to India’s economy

Rising crude oil prices and higher import costs could squeeze margins for Indian refiners, including BPCL, as geopolitical tensions reshape global oil trade and energy markets.

items.summary: India has begun building a green hydrogen ecosystem, but high costs, weak demand and infrastructure gaps need to be addressed soon for the National Green Hydrogen Mission to scale beyond pilot projects.

Musk also reiterated that humanoid robot Optimus could become Tesla's biggest product as the EV maker doubles down on AI and robotics.

items.title: Boarding call: Can Adani succeed where so many airlines have failed?
items.summary: Adani Group's potential airline entry could reshape India's aviation industry, but challenging the IndiGo-Air India duopoly will demand years of investment. Strong airport infrastructure, deep financial resources and aviation synergies offer advantages, while aircraft shortages, regulatory hurdles and tough airline economics remain the biggest obstacles.

items.title: Caliber Mining & Logistics IPO listing tomorrow. Here's what GMP signals ahead of debut

items.summary: China's largest memory chipmaker, ChangXin Memory Technologies (CXMT), is set to debut on the Shanghai Stock Exchange after raising $8.6 billion in Asia's biggest IPO this year, highlighting the success of China's state-backed push to build domestic semiconductor champions and AI infrastructure.

items.title: Coal remains backbone of India's power supply despite renewable push, govt tells Parliament
items.summary: Coal plants supplied nearly seventy percent of India's electricity during summer months. These plants generated seventy-five percent of total power during evening peak demand hours. Government encourages energy efficiency measures and consumption pattern changes to manage rising demand. Peak electricity demand increased twelve percent due to El Nino conditions this year. India's energy shortage declined to zero percent, showing improved supply adequacy.

items.title: PVR Inox Q1 Results: Co swings to black, posts Rs 56 crore profit vs year-ago loss
items.summary: PVR INOX reported a strong June quarter with a net profit of Rs 56.5 crore. Revenue from operations increased to Rs 1,622.2 crore, showing significant year-on-year growth. Admissions rose eight percent while average ticket prices also saw an increase. The company achieved net cash positive status after debt reduction and merger completion. PVR INOX anticipates a robust content pipeline for the remainder of FY27.

items.title: Over 10,000 km of high-speed corridors awarded nationally, 4,809 km completed: Govt
items.summary: Over ten thousand kilometers of high-speed corridors have been awarded nationwide.  Nearly five thousand kilometers of these corridors are now completed and operational.  Another five thousand kilometers of highway projects are currently under active implementation.  Rajasthan leads with the most completed high-speed corridor length.  Various government initiatives are streamlining project execution and land acquisition processes.

items.title: Radhakishan Damani picks up 2% stake in this NBFC stock, which fell 17% in 2026. Do you own it?

items.summary: Both segments - food delivery and quick commerce—are still in varying phases of growth and require investment. Fixed costs generally remain high due to expenses related to expansion, suppressing the Ebitda margin.

items.title: FPIs pump  ₹17,227 crore into Indian stock market in July, ending 4-month outflow streak. Can the comeback sustain?

The company plans significant investments to expand its capacity across various business segments. Revenue is targeted to more than double by 2031, continuing strong annual growth. Stainless steel revenue is projected to increase substantially after expansion completion by 2028. Future demergers are not currently planned as integration remains the focus.

items.title: Explainer: How the Clarity Act could transform crypto regulation in the US

The legislation seeks to clearly define the roles of financial regulators, promote broader adoption of digital assets, and enhance investor protection.

items.title: Supertech twin towers: SC defers hearing on plea seeking refund for homebuyers
items.summary: The Supreme Court has deferred a plea for Supertech twin-towers homebuyers' refunds. A submission on the efforts to satisfy homebuyers not opting for alternative allotments are being considered by the bench.  The court granted a three-month extension to explore further options for these claims.  Homebuyers are reportedly not receiving refunds, with the last payment made in 2024.  The twin-towers in Noida were demolished on August 28, 2022.

He notes that customer retention is crucial for sustaining business amid regulatory changes and retail losses in India's brokerage market.

items.summary: India's poultry industry is expected to return to its long-term growth trajectory of around 10% this fiscal, driven by stronger egg and broiler prices and steady demand, according to Crisil Ratings. Operating margins are also projected to improve by 50-70 basis points after declining last year, despite a 3-5% rise in feed costs.

This premium development aims to meet growing demand from discerning homebuyers.

items.summary: India's real estate sector shows early stabilization signs after a sharp deterioration earlier this year. Global uncertainties continue to weigh on the market but are no longer worsening at the same pace. The residential market is entering a phase of normalisation after sustained growth cycles. Robust office leasing activity and resilient domestic economic fundamentals underpin sector confidence. Future sentiment remains unchanged, suggesting prevailing challenges will persist over six months.

This hiccup leads to prolonged waits for crucial parts, ultimately threatening employment and investments. Additionally, with looming deadlines for India's 2030 solar capacity targets, China's technology export restrictions complicate the landscape, raising costs and delaying the transition to clean energy solutions.

Initiatives such as farm-to-port traceability and the expansion of certified testing labs are essential for ensuring mutual market access and compliance.

items.title: Gratuity, leave encashment not part of CIRP cost: NCLAT
items.summary: The NCLAT dismissed an appeal regarding priority payment of gratuity dues.  It upheld an earlier NCLT order concerning Duncans Industries' former director.  Such dues must be settled according to the company's approved resolution plan.  Gratuity is a terminal benefit, not an insolvency resolution process cost.  The appellate tribunal found the appeal misconceived and dismissed it.

items.title: Seven poll-bound states including UP, Gujarat, Punjab on road to get 25% of NHs

Highway projects signal development pace and priority approvals are expected soon.  The ministry has set targets for awarding and constructing national highways this fiscal year.  This focus aims to boost infrastructure development and influence state election outcomes.

items.title: NaBFID gets nod to raise ₹20,000 cr via Zero-coupon bonds

Investors will receive the full redemption amount after a ten-year maturity period.  The Central Board of Direct Taxes approved the necessary tax framework for this issuance.  NaBFID can issue these bonds until March thirty-first, twenty twenty-eight.

items.title: Iron ore, cement, power output propels June core growth to 5%

Iron ore production surged significantly, while cement and electricity also showed sustained growth. Crude oil and natural gas output remained under pressure during the month. Fertiliser production also continued its decline, marking four consecutive months of contraction.

India's trade policy review is scheduled for July 21 and 23, covering recent economic performance. The review will assess India's resilience and sustained high growth rates post-pandemic. Numerous questions and statements are expected during this important WTO meeting.
